trust.body: CivikSutra is for education only. Confirm dates, rolls, and rules on
trust.footer: We do not endorse any party or candidate.

journey.label: Voter journey (seven guided stages)
journey.descEligibility: Step 1 — Confirm you meet the constitutional voting requirements (citizenship, age, residency).
journey.descRegister: Step 2 — Get your name on the electoral roll and apply for a Voter ID (EPIC) card.
journey.descResearch: Step 3 — Research candidates contesting in your constituency, including affidavits and background.
journey.descCompare: Step 4 — Compare party manifestos side-by-side across key policy areas before deciding.
journey.descLocate: Step 5 — Find your assigned polling booth, get directions, and check accessibility features.
journey.descVote: Step 6 — Run through the booth checklist, EVM/VVPAT procedure, and what to carry on poll day.
journey.descChat: Step 7 — Ask the AI coach anything about the Indian election process; cited and neutral.

calendar.title: Key election dates (reminders)
calendar.subtitle: Illustrative dates — always confirm the official ECI and state election commission schedule, then adjust these Google Calendar links.
calendar.ev1Details: Verify your name on the roll and note Form 6 deadlines for your state (example slot — replace with current CEO dates).
calendar.ev2Details: Carry one approved photo ID, arrive in good time, and follow Model Code of Conduct. Replace with your official polling day.
calendar.ev3Details: Follow eci.gov.in and state CEO sites for final certified results. Replace with the official count schedule.

manifesto.title: Compare Party Manifestos
manifesto.subtitle: Side-by-side analysis of party promises across key policy areas.

voter.title: Be Prepared to Vote
voter.subtitle: Everything you need to know before, during, and after voting.

voter.notEligible: You are not eligible to vote

chat.noWebCitations: This reply has no web sources attached. For official requirements and dates, use the ECI, Electoral Search, and NVSP links in the notice above.
